title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Contract Lens: Field Semantics
summary: A browser data panel negotiates JSON and CSV, receives compressed content, and must validate unknown fields before rendering an aria-live update. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts field semantics increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch representation negotiation and validation boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
